citation: Corfu Channel (United Kingdom v Albania) [1949] ICJ Rep 4
text: Corfu Channel (United Kingdom v Albania) was an ICJ case decided in 1949. The Court addressed Albania's responsibility after British warships struck mines in the Corfu Channel. The judgment is commonly cited for state responsibility, circumstantial evidence, territorial sovereignty, innocent passage, and the duty of a state not to knowingly allow its territory to be used for acts contrary to the rights of other states. The Court rejected the United Kingdom's unilateral minesweeping operation in Albanian waters as a violation of Albanian sovereignty.

case_name: Military and Paramilitary Activities in and against Nicaragua (Nicaragua v United States)
citation: Military and Paramilitary Activities in and against Nicaragua (Nicaragua v United States) [1986] ICJ Rep 14
text: Nicaragua v United States was an ICJ merits judgment issued in 1986. The Court discussed the prohibition on the use of force, non-intervention, customary international law, and self-defence. It treated an armed attack as a grave form of force and distinguished it from lesser unlawful uses of force or intervention. The case is a landmark authority for Article 2(4), Article 51, and customary rules on collective self-defence.

case_name: Oil Platforms (Islamic Republic of Iran v United States of America)
citation: Oil Platforms (Islamic Republic of Iran v United States of America) [2003] ICJ Rep 161
text: Oil Platforms was an ICJ judgment issued in 2003. The Court examined whether United States attacks on Iranian oil platforms could be justified as measures of self-defence. The judgment is commonly cited for necessity and proportionality in self-defence and for the requirement to connect defensive force to an armed attack.
